articles.title: Google’s Gemini AI can answer your questions with 3D models and simulations
articles.summary: Google's Gemini AI now generates interactive 3D models and simulations in response to user questions, allowing real-time manipulation through sliders and adjustable parameters.

articles.title: The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential
articles.summary: Major AI companies face a monetization cliff as they must become profitable before capital runs out. OpenAI and Anthropic exemplify the industry's struggle to transform massive investments into sustainable business models.

articles.title: Google makes it easy to deepfake yourself
articles.summary: YouTube Shorts launches an AI feature allowing creators to generate realistic digital clones of themselves for videos. The rollout highlights YouTube's complex approach to AI-generated content adoption.

articles.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
articles.summary: Florida's Attorney General is investigating OpenAI after ChatGPT allegedly helped plan a shooting at Florida State University that killed two and injured five. The victim's family plans to sue OpenAI.

articles.title: After data breach, $10B valued startup Mercor is having a month
articles.summary: Mercor, a $10B startup, faces lawsuits and customer losses following a data breach. The security incident is significantly impacting the company's business operations.

articles.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
articles.summary: Meta AI app surged to #5 on the App Store following its Muse Spark model launch, up from #57 previously, showing strong user adoption growth.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS with a platform that has attracted 2 million developers without marketing.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges up to $200/month for AI coding assistance, but Goose offers identical functionality as a free, open-source alternative that runs locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and coding challenges. The creative recruitment stunt helped the startup attract top engineering talent in a competitive market.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iranian government-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers in factories, water treatment centers, and refineries. Multiple US agencies issued urgent warnings about the advanced persistent threat group's operations.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russia's military intelligence hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers across 120 countries to redirect users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage campaigns.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a popular AI tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, poses security risks by requiring broad system access. A recently patched vulnerability highlights dangers of giving AI agents extensive permissions across apps and platforms.

articles.title: The Download: AstroTurf wars and exponential AI growth
articles.summary: Americans have dramatically increased synthetic turf installation from 7 million to 79 million square meters since 2001, raising environmental concerns about microplastics and pollution despite industry claims of safety.

articles.title: Is fake grass a bad idea? The AstroTurf wars are far from over.
articles.summary: Cornell University installed synthetic turf for its field hockey field, replacing a meadow ecosystem. Environmental advocates question the environmental impact of artificial grass, sparking ongoing debate.

articles.title: Desalination technology, by the numbers
articles.summary: Desalination technology, which converts seawater to fresh water, is increasingly vital in water-stressed regions like the Middle East. Qatar relies on desalination for 77% of its fresh water and 99% of drinking water.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Waypoint-1.5 enables high-fidelity interactive 3D worlds on standard GPUs. The advancement democratizes immersive simulation technology for broader accessibility and practical applications.

articles.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers introduces multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling AI systems to process and compare text, images, and other data types together for improved search and ranking capabilities.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve while performing tasks on the job. This approach allows agents to adapt and enhance their capabilities through real-world experience rather than just pre-training.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of artificial intelligence, exploring emerging trends and developments in the AI field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creative capabilities in content handling and marketing initiatives.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft releases open-source toolkit for future farming technologies, enabling developers to build AI-powered agricultural solutions for sustainable and efficient farming practices.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google launches Flex and Priority inference tiers for Gemini API to help developers balance cost efficiency with latency requirements.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling high-quality video generation at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to help protect Brazil's forests from deforestation and environmental threats.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source model designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live voice model improves precision and reduces latency for more natural, fluid voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI manipulation risks in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to protect people from harmful AI-driven manipulation.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family with MoE architecture.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, an AI-powered graphics technology that enhances video game photorealism in real-time up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting conditions to generate enhanced details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: The podcast discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, Cursor's coding automations, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: Agentic AI’s governance challenges under the EU AI Act in 2026
articles.summary: AI agents risk creating governance challenges as they can act without clear audit trails, making regulatory compliance difficult. The EU AI Act enforcement in 2026 will impose penalties for inadequate AI governance, particularly in high-risk applications.

articles.title: Anthropic keeps new AI model private after it finds thousands of external vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview AI model discovered thousands of cybersecurity vulnerabilities across major operating systems and browsers. Rather than release it publicly, the company shared the model with 50+ tech organizations through Project Glasswing to address security risks responsibly.

articles.title: AI’s software development success and central management needs
articles.summary: AI adoption is rapidly moving into production phases across enterprises, but governance and integration lag behind. Companies must implement proper controls and integrate AI systems with existing platforms to manage risks effectively.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, advancing debugging of collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture addressing robot navigation challenges in complex indoor environments by handling localization, self-positioning, and path planning tasks.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, the sophisticated engineering systems built around them are emerging as the true competitive advantage for AI companies.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to harvest credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, addressing a longstanding challenge in AI models.

articles.title: Sigmoid vs ReLU Activation Functions: The Inference Cost of Losing Geometric Context
articles.summary: Sigmoid activation functions compress inputs into a narrow range, losing geometric context and spatial information needed for effective deep learning. ReLU preserves magnitude information, enabling deeper networks to remain expressive with less compute.

articles.title: A Coding Guide to Build Advanced Document Intelligence Pipelines with Google LangExtract, OpenAI Models, Structured Extraction, and Interactive Visualization
articles.summary: Tutorial on using Google's LangExtract with OpenAI models to extract structured data from unstructured documents like contracts and meeting notes, with visualization and analytics capabilities.

articles.title: Google AI Research Introduces PaperOrchestra: A Multi-Agent Framework for Automated AI Research Paper Writing
articles.summary: Google Cloud AI Research introduces PaperOrchestra, a multi-agent system that automatically converts raw research notes and experimental data into submission-ready LaTeX manuscripts with literature reviews and verified citations.

articles.title: Home Depot will give you a free power tool with this DeWalt deal - what to know
articles.summary: Home Depot offers a DeWalt string trimmer and leaf blower bundle with a free power tool or battery choice. Great deal for spring lawn care projects.

articles.title: Samsung S95H vs. Samsung S95F: I compared both OLED TVs, and there's a surprise winner
articles.summary: Samsung's 2026 S95H OLED TV is compared to the previous S95F model to determine if the refresh offers enough improvements to warrant an upgrade from last year's flagship.

articles.title: I tested CuerdOS and its unique app lineup is even better than its blazing speed
articles.summary: CuerdOS is a Debian-based distro offering a refreshing alternative approach to preinstalled software. Its unique app lineup impresses beyond its fast performance.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Agentic AI Design Patterns
articles.summary: The article outlines key design patterns for building effective agentic AI systems, providing a roadmap for developers to master autonomous AI agent development and implementation.

articles.title: A Hands-On Guide to Testing Agents with RAGAs and G-Eval
articles.summary: Guide to testing AI agents using RAGAs and G-Eval frameworks for evaluation. Covers hands-on methods for assessing agent performance and reliability in production environments.

articles.title: Handling Race Conditions in Multi-Agent Orchestration
articles.summary: Race conditions occur when multiple agents simultaneously write to the same resource, producing corrupted or nonsensical results. The article addresses preventing this conflict in multi-agent systems.

articles.title: A Visual Explanation of Linear Regression
articles.summary: A comprehensive visual guide with over 100 illustrations explaining linear regression, covering model building, quality measurement, and improvement techniques.

articles.title: How Visual-Language-Action (VLA) Models Work
articles.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models combine visual perception and language understanding to enable humanoid robots to perform tasks. These models use mathematical foundations to integrate multimodal inputs for improved robotic action and decision-making.

articles.title: A Survival Analysis Guide with Python: Using Time-To-Event Models to Forecast Customer Lifetime
articles.summary: Learn survival analysis techniques using Python to model customer retention through Kaplan-Meier curves and Cox Proportional Hazard regressions for lifetime forecasting.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights NVIDIA's breakthroughs in physical AI and robotics. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models enable faster real-world deployment across agriculture, manufacturing, and energy sectors.

articles.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
articles.summary: Samson: A Tyndalston Story from Liquid Swords launches on GeForce NOW, enabling cloud streaming of this mythic narrative game across various devices.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI execution across devices, from RTX PCs to data centers, enabling efficient on-device agentic AI with real-time context access.

articles.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock regularly releases new foundation model versions with improved capabilities and safety. Users should test models before migration to ensure performance and compatibility.

articles.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
articles.summary: AWS Agent Registry, now in preview through Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, enables enterprises to discover, share, and reuse agents and tools across organizations, addressing visibility, control, and duplicate work challenges at scale.

articles.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore introduces BrowserLiveView component for React apps, enabling real-time visibility into AI agent browsing activities. Users can now monitor and control autonomous web interactions through embedded live video feeds.

articles.title: Running Large-Scale GPU Workloads on Kubernetes with Slurm
articles.summary: Slurm, an open-source cluster management system used by 65% of TOP500 systems, helps organizations run large-scale GPU workloads on Kubernetes while preserving existing job scripts and policies.

articles.title: Cut Checkpoint Costs with About 30 Lines of Python and NVIDIA nvCOMP
articles.summary: NVIDIA's nvCOMP enables LLM checkpoint cost reduction using just 30 lines of Python. The tool compresses massive model snapshots, addressing storage challenges during large-scale training.

articles.title: How to Accelerate Protein Structure Prediction at Proteome-Scale
articles.summary: This article discusses accelerating protein structure prediction at proteome-scale, focusing on how proteins interact in complexes rather than functioning individually, representing quaternary protein structure complexity.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how generational differences shape consumer attitudes toward social media ads across various platforms, exploring people's relationships with advertising.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta committed to achieving net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly to reduce its environmental impact.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background leveraged unique perspectives to pioneer innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how unconventional paths can drive breakthrough advancements in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers develop methods to identify and understand interactions at scale in Large Language Models, advancing interpretability by analyzing feature attribution and decision-making processes for safer AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses an information estimator to quantify measurement quality based on noisy data and noise models for optimized optical system design.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, avoiding TD's scalability issues and performing well on long-horizon tasks.
